pr.title: Convert part of player.s to inline ASM with a matched function that simply needs data match
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Moved the first block of melee/pl/player.s into a new src/melee/pl/player.c as mostly inline PowerPC asm, added src/melee/pl/player.h with an opaque StaticPlayer definition and prototypes, renamed several player functions from func_80031xxx-style labels to Player_* symbols, and updated assembly callsites across gm, db, gr, lb, text_2, and text_4 to call the new names. One small wrapper, Player_80031DA8, was written in C and calls func_800EED50; Player_GetPtrForSlot has a C version guarded by NON_MATCHING with a note that it matches once data is moved over. The PR also adjusted build inclusion via obj_files.mk and moved a block of .sdata labels out of asm/sdata.s into asm/melee/lb/code_8000F9F8.s, apparently to preserve or improve data ordering for matching.

key_files.role: New C translation unit containing inline asm versions of Player_GetPtrForSlot, Player_80031790, Player_80031848, Player_80031900, Player_800319C4, Player_80031AD0, Player_80031CB0, Player_80031D2C, plus a C wrapper Player_80031DA8.
New player header with StaticPlayer as an opaque u8[0xE90] struct and public prototypes for the renamed Player_* functions.
Removed roughly 495 lines of the original player assembly block after migrating it to src/melee/pl/player.c; remaining asm calls were updated to Player_800319C4.

Renamed unknown player function comments to Player_* names and added void prototypes for helper calls needed by player.c, including func_80068E98, func_800855C8, func_8008578C, func_800867E8, func_8008688C, and func_800EED50.
Added $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/pl/player.o before the existing asm/melee/pl/player.o so the new C object participates in the link.
Removed a leading block of .sdata labels lbl_804D3740 through lbl_804D37A0.
Reintroduced the removed .sdata labels inside the lb assembly file, including strings such as jobj.h, jobj, lobj.h, lobj, lbarq.c, rp, handle, 0, p->size, and %s :.
Updated many branch-and-link targets from func_80031xxx names to Player_80031DA8, Player_80031848, Player_80031AD0, and Player_80031790.
Updated func_80031790 and func_80031900 calls to Player_80031790 and Player_80031900.
Updated calls to Player_80031CB0 and Player_80031D2C.
Updated calls to Player_80031DA8 and Player_80031790.

what_changed: Created src/melee/pl/player.c and moved the initial player.s function block into it as inline asm functions using nofralloc.
Introduced Player_CheckSlot(s32 slot, u32 line) as an inline C helper that reports "cant get player struct! %d\n" and asserts with __FILE__, a line value, and "0" when the slot check fails.
Added a guarded C implementation of Player_GetPtrForSlot under #ifdef NON_MATCHING with the comment "Matches when everything is moved over"; the active fallback remains an inline asm body.
Added a fully C Player_80031DA8 wrapper that forwards param_1 and param_2 to func_800EED50.

Renamed player functions in declarations and assembly callsites from raw func_80031724, func_80031790, func_80031848, func_80031900, func_800319C4, func_80031AD0, func_80031CB0, func_80031D2C, and func_80031DA8 to Player_GetPtrForSlot and Player_80031xxx names.
Added src/melee/pl/player.h with StaticPlayer defined as an opaque struct containing u8 data[0xE90] and prototypes for the new Player_* symbols.
Added src/melee/pl/player.o to obj_files.mk immediately before asm/melee/pl/player.o.
Moved .sdata labels lbl_804D3740 through lbl_804D37A0 from global asm/sdata.s into asm/melee/lb/code_8000F9F8.s.
Added or concretized function prototypes in include/functions.h for helper functions called by the new player.c inline asm/C code.
smart_moves: Used inline asm as an intermediate decomp step, reducing standalone assembly while preserving exact codegen for functions that were not yet fully translated.
Kept the old assembly instruction comments and labels inside player.c, making the migration reviewable against the deleted player.s block and preserving address-level searchability.
Renamed all known callsites to the new Player_* symbols in the same PR, avoiding mixed func_80031xxx and Player_* references.
Introduced an opaque StaticPlayer with the known stride size, 0xE90, so pointer arithmetic and function signatures could be expressed without needing a complete player struct layout.
Isolated the potentially matchable C version of Player_GetPtrForSlot behind NON_MATCHING instead of forcing an unready C translation into the build.
Handled data-order side effects by relocating the affected .sdata block rather than leaving global sdata layout changes implicit.
Added the new C object before the remaining asm player object, which is important when splitting a module while preserving link order and symbol ownership.
decomp_lessons: When converting only part of an asm file, add the new C object adjacent to the remaining asm object in obj_files.mk to preserve link order as much as possible.
For partial struct knowledge, an opaque byte-array struct can be sufficient to express globals, function returns, and array stride while delaying field naming until evidence improves.
Rename callsites in assembly as soon as functions get subsystem ownership; even if the implementation remains inline asm, Player_* symbols are more searchable and communicate module boundaries.
A function can be marked as logically matched but still blocked by data placement; keep the C candidate guarded and document the dependency, as done by the Player_GetPtrForSlot NON_MATCHING block.
Moving code from .s to inline asm can expose data-order problems; inspect .sdata/.sdata2 placement and be ready to move data labels to the translation unit that owns the order.
Small wrapper functions are good early C wins: Player_80031DA8 reduced to a direct func_800EED50 call while larger nearby routines stayed inline asm.
naming_conventions: Player_GetPtrForSlot was used for the previously anonymous func_80031724, showing a preference for a descriptive name once behavior is known.

Less-understood player routines were renamed with a subsystem prefix plus address suffix, such as Player_80031790, Player_80031848, Player_80031900, Player_800319C4, Player_80031AD0, Player_80031CB0, Player_80031D2C, and Player_80031DA8.
The player header used include guard _player_h_, matching the simple lowercase filename style present in this early codebase slice.
The temporary/opaque struct name StaticPlayer was used for the player data array at lbl_80453080 with size 0xE90.
Parameter names in partially understood prototypes stayed generic where behavior was not documented, e.g. param_1, param_2, id, and slot.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inline asm functions were declared with asm return/type syntax and nofralloc to preserve original prologues/epilogues.
Original address comments were retained in player.c, enabling byte-for-byte comparison with the deleted asm/melee/pl/player.s contents.
The active Player_GetPtrForSlot remained inline asm while a C candidate was guarded by NON_MATCHING, signaling that data layout/order rather than code logic was the remaining blocker.
The PR updated branch targets in assembly files without changing instructions, e.g. bl func_80031724 became bl Player_GetPtrForSlot and bl func_80031DA8 became bl Player_80031DA8.
Data labels were moved wholesale from asm/sdata.s into asm/melee/lb/code_8000F9F8.s, preserving labels and contents while changing ownership/location.
The migrated routines kept references to raw labels such as lbl_80453080, lbl_803BCDC0, lbl_803BCDE0, lbl_803BCE44, lbl_803BCE60, and lbl_804D3940 where stronger names were not yet available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace inline asm in src/melee/pl/player.c with C implementations.
Find later PRs that define fields for StaticPlayer instead of u8 data[0xE90].
Find later PRs that resolve the Player_GetPtrForSlot data match blocker mentioned by the NON_MATCHING comment.
Search for subsequent renames of Player_80031790, Player_80031848, Player_80031900, Player_800319C4, Player_80031AD0, Player_80031CB0, Player_80031D2C, and Player_80031DA8.
Search for later cleanup of lbl_80453080 and the moved lbl_804D3740-lbl_804D37A0 sdata labels.
